what: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: Consolidated maintainer reframes + codex design rounds 1–2. Doctrine (settled): batchit dispatches delivers outputs; holds staleness opinion . Correctness + ergonomics . Baseline batchit 26.7.19 (HEAD 235174f).

what: 0. Normative doctrine invariant (codex-supplied; test around it)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: batchit launch decision may depend marker existence contents. Marker reads permitted completing verifying already-dispatched commit, hand consumer prior details (§7). marker carries batchit-computed input hash, code hash, mtime, provenance, “need--run” state — protocol number, random attempt token, committed output names, (§7) OPAQUE consumer-supplied details value batchit ascribes meaning . lockdown test asserts batchit never branches dispatch marker state.

what: 1. The reframe — dispatch is a function on two orthogonal axes
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: Envelope meta carries: - fn_kind — "package" (package+symbol+body/formals hash; hash-verified ends; auditable; production stages) "adhoc" (serialized closure; static-lint gated; ad-hoc ). - outputs (optional) — absent → return value (today’s batch_run, collect-gated); present → declared-output commit (§3), result small commit record, never raw data. Independent axes; discriminated envelope codex required. .BATCH_PROTOCOL bumps; old executor rejects new-shaped envelope (existing check R/batch.R:258); worker checks protocol conditionally loading consumer package.

title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: batch_run(target, items, …) — unchanged (package fn, return value). batch_task(fn, items, outputs, style, …) — package|adhoc fn + declared-output commit. collect. fn = batch_target() descriptor (→ package) bare closure (→ adhoc). batch_fn(fn, items, …) — adhoc fn + return value. Distinct public contracts; shared internal engine.

what: 3.1 Outputs spec (per item; never in target args) + conservative path validation
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: outputs = named character vector c(<name> = <final path>). Lives meta, never args. -ends validation, conservatively scoped (codex 5): - non-empty; unique names; unique paths; absolute; normalizePath(mustWork=FALSE)-normalized. - destination absent existing non-directory, non-symlink file (dirs + symlinks rejected; base R portably detect FIFO/socket/device special file, destination rejected may replaced commit rename — documented, guarantee). - parent dir output/marker/stage path must already exist. - within ONE invocation, reject output path aliases another (item concurrent item) — normalize .., resolve existing symlinks, check hardlink identity platform exposes . Explicitly promised: cross-invocation/cross-process exclusivity (global lock), case-fold/Unicode canonicalization arbitrary mounts, absent path later become alias. Contract assumes single exclusive writer per output; TOCTOU validate rename documented boundary.

what: 3.2 Two styles (external_writer dropped) — BOTH IMPLEMENTED (Unit 1 return, Unit 2 staged_writer)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: return codec qs2, always. arbitrary writer closures spec. staged_writer‘s scoped accessor backed package-level environment (.batch_stage_env), entered/exited around .call .batch_execute() (never inside .batch_commit_task(), since paths must exist target runs) — see batch_stage_path() .batch_stage_paths_for() R/batch_task.R. parent-side sweep (.batch_sweep_task_temps()) matches .<attempt>.tmp .<attempt>.stage, covering styles’ leftover temps one SIGKILL/timeout cleanup path.

what: 3.3 Commit sequence — child-side, in .batch_execute after do.call (codex 4 order)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: Run target; prepare every output temp (return: qs2-write ; staged: assert every batch_stage_path(name) exists regular non-symlink file — undeclared/absent name → error, zero renames). Prepare + close marker temp, embedding fresh random attempt token (+ protocol, committed names, §7 details). Verify every temp (outputs + marker) regular non-symlink file. Remove old final marker verify removal succeeded (stale marker must survive attempt — §7 read step 0 already captured prior details). Replace every final output (rename temp → final). Rename marker temp → final marker path LAST (atomic commit point — last namespace op). Read back final marker verify attempt token; emit commit record. Cleanup unconditional every failure path (target error staged files; partial return serialization; failure mid output-rename loop; marker temp/rename failure; timeout/kill far parent can reach): remove remaining temps; never roll back guessing old finals recoverable. Preparing everything step 4 keeps invalid window minimal stops serialization/marker failure needlessly destroying prior valid witness. Replacement primitive: file.rename() overwrite semantics differ across POSIX/Windows/CIFS; unlink--rename opens missing-file window. Contract states supported semantics = POSIX + server-side-atomic CIFS rename-replace; platform-atomic-replace helper supplied/tested, production target (uppsala + CIFS) tested path.

title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: Returns exact per-output sibling temp path batchit rename . Backed scoped option set .call duration (set/restore + unconditional cleanup). Rejects undeclared name; errors outside staged run; partial stages cleaned failure.

title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: commit record echoes named output map actually committed: list(committed = <named char: name -> final path>, attempt = <token>). parent inspector compares committed (names paths) + attempt dispatched outputs + token issued — bare name list. raw value ever crosses back (batch_task collect; target return unconditionally discarded child-side commit, styles).

title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: fn_kind REQUIRED ("package"|"adhoc"); unknown/missing → reject ends. package: package+symbol+hash required; dev_path valid (names CONSUMER’s tree). adhoc: closure (meta$fn) + per-dispatch nonce (meta$nonce) required; package/symbol/hash (forbidden, directions — package-kind envelope carrying fn/nonce rejected ); worker preflight conditional fn_kind (inst/batch_worker.R’s .batch_worker_check() requires .function(meta$fn) adhoc instead package/symbol/hash); formals + args get -end validation package items (every arg named formal; positional; ... prohibited, targets) via .batch_validate_adhoc_item(). dev_path adhoc names BATCHIT’S tree (consumer identity exists validate ) — resolved decision, see §5 note . style required EXACTLY outputs present; forbidden otherwise. styles (return, staged_writer) work either fn_kind — style fn_kind independent axes, designed. Unknown envelope fields rejected, ignored.

what: 5. adhoc + the static self-containedness LINT (not proof) — IMPLEMENTED (Unit 3)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: codetools::findGlobals(fn, merge=FALSE) — inspect $variables $functions. Reject global {base/implicit-base + base operators, pkg::/pkg::: qualified reference}. Implemented .batch_lint_adhoc_fn() (R/batch_adhoc.R), applied ends: frontend (batch_fn() / batch_task() bare closure) dispatch time, .batch_check_envelope() CHILD. Verified empirically: pkg::fun()/pkg:::fun() calls NEVER independently flagged codetools (::/::: call reported, base), special-casing needed qualified-call carve-. Mandatory rebase (accepted) closure’s environment baseenv() serialization — simpler “optional,” closes large/secret enclosing-environment carriage path. (env-preservation mode; ever needed loud documented adhoc limitation.) Implemented .batch_rebase_adhoc_closure(); CHILD re-rebases defensively, right .call(), case hand-crafted envelope reached worker closure never rebased. Verified empirically: un-rebased closure’s original environment (anything bound ) round-trips qs2 wire INTACT (proof carriage path closes); baseenv()-rooted closure instead reconnects RECEIVING session’s baseenv(). Closure-acceptance (primitive explicitly rejected — real formals/body/environment lint rebase); ... prohibited; pkg::fun runs whatever installed child (version claim). Narrowed promise: best-effort static lint rejecting directly detectable unqualified global references — prove behavioral closure, hermetic execution, portability, dependency identity (blind spots: get/mget/assign/string .call, eval(parse()), substitute, .GlobalEnv, formula/attr envs, <<-, S4/R5/R6 dispatch, ambient state). Ad-hoc ; production stays fn_kind="package". Proven closure using get("x", envir = environment()): passes lint (get/environment base), yet fails RUN time real child subprocess rebase severed environment get() otherwise walked. codetools added DESCRIPTION Imports. batch_fn(fn, items, n_workers, dev_path = NULL, collect = FALSE, ...) — IMPLEMENTED: adhoc, return-value sibling batch_run(), transport/worker/-ends validation, R/batch_adhoc.R. Adhoc result identity (§9.4) — IMPLEMENTED: since adhoc envelope carries package/symbol/hash, .batch_inspect_result()/_impl() gained expected_nonce argument; non-NULL replaces package-identity check tgt$fn_kind == "adhoc" && tgt$nonce == expected_nonce (id already checked, unconditionally, earlier function). nonce fresh per-item token generator batch_task()’s commit attempt token (.batch_new_attempt_token(), reused, duplicated). batch_task() + adhoc, attempt (commit-record witness, unaffected fn_kind) nonce (adhoc envelope identity) issued — redundant: attempt binds MARKER/commit record; nonce binds ENVELOPE’s executed-code identity, exactly mirroring hash job fn_kind = "package". Resolved ambiguity — dev_path adhoc: design text silent dev_path mean “consumer” identity validate . Resolved : dev_path fn_kind = "adhoc" ALWAYS names BATCHIT’S source tree (validated via .batch_validate_dev_path(dev_path, "batchit"), worker’s runner-load step treats given adhoc dev_path way package-kind runner == consumer self-test branch treats ). lets batchit’s adhoc test suite run source without reinstall; second dev-tree slot helper package closure’s pkg::fun() might reference — resolve via ordinary lazy namespace loading worker regardless (pre-loading step needed, verified: R’s :: operator loads -yet-loaded namespace ). downstream consumer’s batch_fn() calls pass dev_path = NULL practice, getting installed batchit. Resolved: .BATCH_PROTOCOL bump Unit 3. envelope schema change purely additive (fn/nonce new OPTIONAL meta fields); old (pre-Unit-3) worker already rejects fn_kind = "adhoc" loudly structurally, code loads (.batch_worker_check()’s hardcoded copy check), existing protocol number already provides version-skew safety net Unit 1’s bump . Confirmed needing touch expect_identical(batchit:::.BATCH_PROTOCOL, 2L) pin test-batch_task.R.

title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: batchit-computed fingerprints, plan records, provenance sidecars, task_scope_id, content hashing, batchit DECIDING skip. §7’s skip CONSUMER-decided; batchit transports record.

what: 7. Opt-in consumer skip — .batchit record + batch_prior()/batch_skip() (maintainer-approved 2026-07-20; TTE STAYS SKIP-FREE) — REMOVED 2026-07-23
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: SUPERSEDED: entire mechanism REMOVED — see PUBLIC_API.md §5. consumer (TTE skip-free doctrine), batch_record()/batch_prior()/batch_skip(), details/skipped fields, prior-marker read deleted; commit engine back pre-skip {protocol, attempt, committed} marker / {committed, attempt} result shape. section retained historical design record Unit 4 built. Build OPT-; production s1/s2/s3 pass skip logic always recompute (Phase 5′ unchanged). - Marker .batchit = commit record + OPTIONAL consumer details value (§0 invariant: opaque batchit; batchit never launches ). - batch_record(details) — fn attaches small serializable details item’s new marker. IMPLEMENTED: exported, R/batch_task.R. Backed .batch_record_env, scoped accessor entered/ exited TIGHTLY around .call() .batch_execute() (mirrors .batch_stage_env exactly). Last call wins (§9.3); errors called outside active batch_task() target run. - batch_prior() — returns PRIOR marker’s details (captured step 0 removal), NULL. fn inspects . IMPLEMENTED: exported, R/batch_task.R. Step 0 .batch_read_prior_marker() (child-side, .call()), reads final marker EXACTLY accepts prior decodes protocol/attempt-token/committed-output-map verify item’s declared outputs (.batch_valid_marker_record()) — malformed, foreign, absent, directory, symlinked marker become prior = NULL. valid prior whose details NULL/absent also makes batch_prior() return NULL (skip disabled), exactly specified §9.2. - batch_skip() — fn RETURNS sentinel → batchit re-commit: verifies prior marker + every declared output still exists (marker vouches fully committed), records done, preserves , writes new marker. Reachable batch_prior() non-NULL. IMPLEMENTED: exported sentinel constructor (errors outside active target run, like two accessors); .batch_execute() detects sentinel (.batch_is_skip()) .call() calls .batch_commit_task_skip() INSTEAD .batch_commit_task() — re-derives witness fresh (re-reads + re-verifies marker, re-stats every declared output regular non-symlink file), never trusting step-0 snapshot. NULL prior, anything longer verifies/exists, fails loud marker left completely untouched (§9.2 point 4). - Doctrine (reconciled §0): batchit READS marker (serve batch_prior / verify commit) HONOURS batch_skip, NEVER decides skip NEVER branches launch marker. details consumer’s — weak details mis-judges currency consumer’s bug, exactly like swereg’s rawbatch/skeleton caches. Safer deleted s1 resume: marker atomic commit point, mtime/existence heuristic. - Commit-record shape change: commit record gained third, ALWAYS-present field — list(committed, attempt, skipped). skipped = FALSE ordinary commit (attempt freshly-dispatched token, unchanged behaviour); skipped = TRUE skip--reuse (attempt PRIOR marker’s token, since nothing new committed — construction can never equal token dispatch just issued). .batch_inspect_result()’s parent-side check skipped-aware: committed output map (names + paths) always checked exactly; attempt-token-must-match- dispatch check applies skipped FALSE. protocol bump (reasoning §5’s -bump note — result-envelope shape produced consumed installed version within one call, dispatch-envelope skew concern).

what: 9. Pre-code specifications (codex round-3; design is implementation-ready after these)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: 9.1 Per-item output + marker addressing (P0). - batch_task(fn, items, outputs, style, …): outputs list aligned items — outputs[[]] (named character vector) item ’s output map. Length must equal length(items); items named, outputs may named--item-id instead (set names). - Marker path derived deterministically uniquely per item: file.path(dirname(sort(output_paths)[1]), paste0(".batchit__", item_id)) — lexicographically first output’s directory + item’s already-required unique stable id (two items sharing directory never collide). Overridable via explicit per-item marker path consumer needs one. - Marker paths join invocation-wide collision check (§3.1) every output every marker. parent validates marker pathname + parent directory; NEVER inspects marker existence/type/contents launch (child’s job — §0). 9.2 Step-0 / skip state machine (P1, normative). Per item, child: 1. dispatch, .call, read final marker . 2. Accept prior protocol + attempt token + exact output map decode structurally verify; else prior = NULL. Expose prior$details via batch_prior() (NULL valid prior details absent/NULL — valid marker NULL details therefore disables skip; document ). 3. Leave final marker UNTOUCHED function runs. 4. batch_skip(): require non-NULL valid prior, else error (marker removal); re-read + match marker token & output map re-stat every declared output regular non-symlink file; success record done + preserve, writing new marker; mismatch/missing → fail loud, marker untouched. 5. normal return: run commit steps §3.3 1–7 (step 4 removes old marker — old marker survives every new temp ready). 9.3 Skip cleanup + batch_record (P1). Skip successful early exit, must remove every current-attempt stage/temp file reporting reuse. batch_record() called → last call wins. batch_record() batch_skip() → new details discarded, prior marker unchanged. Unserializable/oversized details → NORMAL commit fails old-marker removal. Scoped accessor state (stage dir, prior, record) restored success, error, skip. 9.4 Ad-hoc result identity (P1). parent inspector today requires package/symbol/hash (R/batch.R:465). Result identity now fn_kind: package results bind package+symbol+hash (unchanged); adhoc results bind dispatch attempt token (id + per-dispatch nonce parent issued), since adhoc envelope package identity. .batch_inspect_result branches fn_kind. 9.5 §0 lockdown tests (normative). Prove: parent scheduling/launch traces IDENTICAL absent / valid / malformed / stale markers; marker read occurs worker dispatch; explicit function-returned batch_skip() sentinel enters reuse handling; batchit never compares interprets details. Plus parse/AST guard dispatch-launch path references marker state.

what: 8. swereg migration (separate phase, after batchit ships; NO skip logic)
title: Phase 6′ design (v3) — batchit function dispatch + output-commit
text: Stage table: s2 FIRST (canary, return, replaces .resume_fresh) → s1c, s1b (return) → s1a, s1d (staged_writer). save_rawbatch later; process_skeletons + s3 scope. : swap hand-rolled write batch_task declared-output call (batch_prior/batch_skip); proven-red (killed mid-commit → marker → item fails loud; wrote-nothing → fails loud); full swereg suite; codex gate; push; CI. Reinstall batchit testing; nothing runs mid-pipeline.

title: batchit public API
text: Status: authoritative public surface (naming v2). Adopted design review, 2026-07-22. document defines public functions batchit exports one . supersedes function NAMES used PHASE6_DESIGN.md (batch_run, batch_task, batch_stream, batch_target, batch_fn, batch_stage_path, batch_record, batch_prior, batch_skip). change MECHANISM PHASE6_DESIGN.md — wire protocol, envelope, atomic commit engine, §0 doctrine invariant unchanged. Names bare qualified-use (batchit::run(...)), batch_ prefix — package name already carries . Names full-sentence self-documenting deliberate choice: batchit exports functions wide (CRAN) audience, name states behavior full.

title: batchit public API
text: batchit brainless muscle: run one function across many argument-sets, one fresh subprocess per set. holds state, decides nothing, knows nothing domain. brain — items run, function, tracking progress — lives consumer (MHT project, swereg’s RegistryStudy R6 class). add stateful “batch” object batchit; shelved provenance program §0 doctrine forbids. item one named argument-set fn — named list whose names match fn’s formals. batchit hands fn arguments checked .call (every element named formal; positional; ...). batchit never looks inside item.

title: batchit public API
text: distinction : can worker read data, must one reader produce ? Shape — normal case. worker opens data. pass complete list items; fn loads computes, running worker. covers every TTE stage: s1, s2, s3, skeleton creation. Shape B — specialist. single sequential source large read independently per worker (55 GB LMED file). One reader — parent — streams hands slices. sole consumer save_rawbatch. Shape norm. Shape B one function one situation. symmetric halves grid; treat Shape B routine choice.

title: batchit public API
text: One fn loads data computes.

title: batchit public API
text: parent produces slice via callback; workers commit files atomically. producer callback, function(id) -> item's argument-set. batchit calls parent, lazily, one item time workers free (bounded queue). producer’s RESULT crosses worker; producer function stays parent may access parent environment freely.

title: batchit public API
text: package_function(package, symbol) — hash-verified descriptor fn. Names package function plus body/formals identity hash, worker refuses code differs parent dispatched. where_to_write_output(name) — called INSIDE style = "staged_writer" target; returns exact sibling temp path write output name , streamed write still goes atomic commit.

what: 3.4 fn: a package function OR a closure
title: batchit public API
text: Every dispatcher’s fn accepts either: package_function(...) descriptor — hash-verified; use production; bare closure — ad-hoc, gated static self-containedness lint mandatory baseenv() rebase; tests one-offs . folds former batch_fn. package-vs-closure choice property fn argument, separate function name.

title: batchit public API
text: per-item wire unit S3 batch_envelope: structure(list(protocol, meta, args), class = "batch_envelope"), validator print method. dispatchers build one envelope per item; child validates . never construct pass envelope. stays plain list wire worker can read one bare qs2:: package loads — trust gate lets worker refuse version-skewed hostile code loading . reason hardcoded structural pre-check inst/batch_worker.R (.batch_worker_check()) stays standalone base-R copy; conformance test asserts agrees S3 validator.

title: batchit public API
text: batch_record / batch_prior / batch_skip (opt-consumer skip) — consumer. TTE stages skip-free doctrine (Phase 5’). Re-add real consumer ever needs . batch_fn separate export — package-vs-closure axis fn argument’s type, needs name . Shape B return-value modes (batch_stream collect) — consumer. Shape B collapses single atomic-write specialist.

title: batchit public API
text: collect longer flag. run returns nothing; run_and_collect returns list length items, item order, NULL-safe (NULL return keeps slot, vanish). outputs — per-item named character vector, c(<name> = <final path>). Commit atomic: .batchit marker renamed place last commit point. See PHASE6_DESIGN.md section 3. style — "return" (target returns list(<name> = <value>) batchit qs2-serializes ) "staged_writer" (target writes output where_to_write_output(<name>)). producer runs PARENT; result crosses worker.

title: batchit public API
text: Rename sweep. Old frontends -> new names; split collect run / run_and_collect; fold batch_fn; delete skip trio; drop Shape B return-value modes. Covers NAMESPACE, roxygen tags, test files, NEWS.md, README.md, PHASE6_DESIGN.md cross-references. Gate: rcmdcheck clean + full test suite green. Build stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically. NEW code: Shape B (mirai/producer) transport plus child-side commit engine Shape atomic path already uses (commit engine reads envelope care item arrived). Proven-red test (killed mid-commit -> marker -> item fails loud). codex gate. batch_envelope S3 wrapper — validator + print; conformance test vs hardcoded .batch_worker_check(). (deferred) single-source envelope schema, constructor -package validator derive one declarative field matrix.

title: batchit public API
text: §0 normative invariant holds: batchit LAUNCH decision depends marker state. caching lives batchit. stateful batch object. See PHASE6_DESIGN.md sections 0 6.

text: Richard Aubrey White. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {batchit: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract},   author = {Richard Aubrey White},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 26.7.20},   url = {https://papadopoulos-lab.github.io/batchit}, }

what: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
title: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
text: One subprocess dispatcher -ends-validated contract. batchit runs one R function across many subprocesses, makes parent/child boundary contract rather assumption. target descriptor — package + symbol + digest(list(body, formals)) identity hash — never closure, child can refuse code differs parent dispatched. Work items validated target’s formals ends. Every result comes back structured envelope (protocol, id, status, value--error, executed-target identity, captured warnings).

title: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
text: one contract two transports, matched two real workload shapes. Shape — run() / run_and_collect(): items already exist; run fresh process per item. Process exit memory strategy memory-bound work (worker peaks tens GB hand memory back OS, reuse wrong thing). Transport: processx, one child per item. run() returns nothing (target writes output); run_and_collect() returns item’s value, item order. fn either package_function() descriptor (hash-verified, production) bare closure (ad-hoc, gated self-containedness lint — tests one-offs). Shape , declared-output variant — run_and_write_files_atomically(): transport, instead value crossing back, item commits declared output files atomically (7-step rename sequence, witnessed per-item marker file) — see PUBLIC_API.md section 3.1. Shape B — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically(): parent producer item payload (data slice), generated lazily bounded backpressure whole dataset never lands memory (disk twice) . Transport: mirai daemons, 2 * n_workers items flight. Delivery via atomic declared-output commit engine run_and_write_files_atomically() — commit record crosses back, never raw value. fn package_function() descriptor (ad-hoc closure support — sole consumer, save_rawbatch, uses package function).
code: t <- batchit::package_function("mypkg", "process_one_slice") out <- batchit::run_and_collect(   t,   items = list(list(x = 1), list(x = 2), list(x = 3)),   n_workers = 3 ) t <- batchit::package_function("mypkg", "write_one_slice") batchit::stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically(   t,   ids = c("2019", "2020", "2021"),   producer = function(id) list(slice = load_year(id)),   outputs = list(     `2019` = c(main = "/data/2019.qs2"),     `2020` = c(main = "/data/2020.qs2"),     `2021` = c(main = "/data/2021.qs2")   ),   n_workers = 4 )

title: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
text: Target = package + symbol + body/formals hash. child re-computes hash loading refuses run different code version dispatched. hash srcref-independent, installed-vs-load_all code identity holds. Every formal must named every item, including optional ones — catches silently-dropped optional argument, just typo. Validation runs ends. Parent = early UX (every item, just first); child = correctness (may loaded different version). positional / duplicate / blank names, ... targets. Result envelope: protocol, id, status, value--structured-error, executed target identity (package + symbol + hash), captured warnings. Atomicity scoped envelope, whatever files target writes. run() drops value entirely — targets write output, status crosses back; run_and_collect() sibling returns item’s value. default mirai profile never touched; stream claims fresh, nonce-namespaced private profile tears . Thread-agnostic: batchit sets BLAS / data.table thread counts. within-item thread policy consumer’s. Runner vs consumer: worker script always runner’s (batchit’s); dev_path, given, consumer’s source tree. worker daemon load packages.

title: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
text: Parallel shape B needs suggested mirai package (parallelism opt-).

title: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract
text: batchit extracted swereg Phases 0–3 one-dispatcher project — contract designed, correctness defects parent/child boundary fixed, every legacy engine routed single dispatcher. See swereg’s PROJECT.md history design rationale. one non-mechanical change extraction runner-vs-consumer loading seam (swereg ; batchit runner).

what: batchit: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract — batchit-package
title: batchit: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract — batchit-package
text: generic parent/child dispatch contract running one R function across many subprocesses. package target descriptor – package plus symbol plus body/formals identity hash, never closure – child can refuse code differs parent dispatched; ad-hoc target dispatches self-contained closure value instead. Work items validated ends target's formals, every result comes back structured envelope (protocol, id, status, value--error, target identity, captured warnings). Two transports share one contract: fresh process per item via 'processx' memory-bound work process exit memory strategy, lazy producer bounded backpressure via 'mirai' daemons work whose items generated demand.

title: batchit: One Subprocess Dispatcher with a Both-Ends-Validated Contract — batchit-package
text: Maintainer: Richard Aubrey White hello@rwhite.(ORCID) Authors: Richard Aubrey White hello@rwhite.(ORCID)

title: Describe a dispatch target — package_function
text: target descriptor, never function object, name, closure: package + symbol + hash function's body formals. Package name plus symbol alone insufficient – development code, installed code cache identity can differ – descriptor also records digest(list(body, formals)) identity hash. child re-computes hash loading target refuses run differs, closes stale-code / wrong-version hole bare package+symbol reference leave.

title: Describe a dispatch target — package_function
Describe a dispatch target — package_function
text: package Package holding target (character scalar). CONSUMER package; need batchit. symbol Name target function package (character scalar). May internal (unexported) symbol – resolved package's namespace. version Optional recorded version; defaults package's installed version. Advisory – hash child actually checks.

title: Describe a dispatch target — package_function
text: package_function descriptor: list class "package_function" elements package, symbol, version, hash, formal_names.

title: Describe a dispatch target — package_function
text: hash deliberately narrow: covers target's body formals . changed helper target calls, namespace constant closes , S4/R6 method table, dependency's version outside – proves "target definition", "provably identical behaviour", latter claimed. utils::removeSource() applied hashing identity independent srcref (comments / whitespace): otherwise installed package (srcref) devtools::load_all() tree (srcref) disagree identical code, exactly happens parent runs installed package worker dev-loads source. target takes ... rejected: arbitrary dots incompatible reliable detection mistyped missing argument contract depends .

title: Describe a dispatch target — package_function
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # target an exported or internal function of any installed package t <- package_function("mypkg", "process_one_slice") t$formal_names } # }

what: Run fn on each of a fixed list of items, one subprocess per item, returning nothing — run
title: Run fn on each of a fixed list of items, one subprocess per item, returning nothing — run
text: collect = FALSE sibling run_and_collect() – shape-transport (see run_and_collect() .batch_run_impl() shared contract details: hash-verified target/adhoc dispatch, -end validation, per-item logs, bounded log tail, loud failure). title: Run fn on each of a fixed list of items, one subprocess per item, returning nothing — run
code: run(   fn,   items,   n_workers,   dev_path = NULL,   p = NULL,   label = NULL,   timeout = .BATCH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT )

title: Run fn on each of a fixed list of items, one subprocess per item, returning nothing — run
text: fn EITHER package_function descriptor package_function() bare closure – see details . items List items; fully-named list fn's formals. Named items keep name item id; unnamed items get index. n_workers Concurrent subprocesses (validated: finite, whole, >= 1). dev_path Source tree devtools::load_all() worker, NULL installed package. package_function() fn CONSUMER's source tree; bare-closure fn batchit's (adhoc closure separate consumer identity load). given--wrong path errors rather silently falling back installed code. p progress callback progressr progressor, NULL. called per completed item message = <id time>. label Optional short stage tag prefixed progress message. timeout Per-item wall-clock limit seconds; worker exceeds killed reported failure. Defaults generous hang-catcher (internal .BATCH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, 6 hours); pass Inf disable.

title: Run fn on each of a fixed list of items, one subprocess per item, collecting values — run_and_collect
text: fn EITHER package_function() descriptor (hash-verified, auditable; use production) bare closure (ad-hoc, gated static self-containedness lint mandatory baseenv() rebase; tests one-offs – folds former ad-hoc-closure frontend). -end validation, hash-verified target descriptor (, bare closure, per-dispatch identity nonce), per-item logs written files (never pipes – chatty worker filling OS pipe buffer deadlocks pipe transport), bounded log tail failure, loud stop first failure. Warnings target captures surfaced parent, tagged item id. batchit thread-agnostic: sets BLAS / data.table thread counts passes none worker. fn multi-threaded, dividing cores across n_workers (avoid oversubscription) CONSUMER's responsibility, runner's. worker script always runner's (batchit's); dev_path, given, CONSUMER's source tree. runner consumer differ, worker loads (consumer via dev_path/requireNamespace, runner via requireNamespace).

what: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
text: declared-output sibling run()/run_and_collect() (design PHASE6_DESIGN.md sections 2-3): transport (fresh subprocess per item via processx, worker script, -ends item validation hash-verified target), instead raw return value crossing back, target's return committed outputs[[]] – named map final file paths – CHILD, via --nothing 7-step rename sequence (.batch_commit_task()). per-item MARKER file atomic witness complete commit – VALID, engine-produced marker (one decodes whose protocol/attempt-token/committed-output-map verify) witness; bare pathname EXISTENCE . target errors old marker removed (commit step 4) leaves unrelated pre-existing marker path completely untouched, file sitting marker path mean attempt committed.

title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
code: run_and_write_files_atomically(   fn,   items,   outputs,   style = "return",   n_workers,   dev_path = NULL,   p = NULL,   label = NULL,   timeout = .BATCH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,   target = NULL )

title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
text: fn EITHER package_function descriptor package_function() (fn_kind = "package") bare closure (fn_kind = "adhoc"): self-contained (base R, pkg::-qualified calls, formals – see .batch_lint_adhoc_fn()), primitive, taking .... items List items; fully-named list fn's formals. Named items keep name item id; unnamed items get index. outputs list aligned items: outputs[[]] item 's output map, named character vector c(<name> = <final path>). May instead NAMED ITEM ID (name set derived item ids, order) items named. Every path must absolute; destination must absent existing non-directory, non-symlink file (base R portably distinguish FIFO/socket/device special file, file rejected MAY replaced commit rename); every output every derived marker path must unique across WHOLE call. style Commit style: "return" (target returns named list – Unit 1) "staged_writer" (target writes output via where_to_write_output() instead – Unit 2). value errors. n_workers Concurrent subprocesses (validated: finite, whole, >= 1). dev_path fn_kind = "package", CONSUMER package's source tree devtools::load_all() worker (NULL installed consumer package). fn_kind = "adhoc" consumer identity, instead names BATCHIT'S source tree (see run()'s dev_path doc) – NULL (default) uses installed batchit. p progress callback progressr progressor, NULL. label Optional short stage tag prefixed progress message. timeout Per-item wall-clock limit seconds; see run(). target Deprecated former name fn (Unit 1/2 originally shipped parameter target = ...). Pass fn instead; supplying errors.

title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
text: list, named item id, item order: element item's commit record, list(committed = <named char: name -> final path>, attempt = <token>). Never target's raw return value.

title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
text: Two commit styles, EITHER fn_kind: style = "return" (Unit 1 – target returns list(<name> = <value>, ...), names matching declared outputs EXACTLY; value qs2-serialized declared path) style = "staged_writer" (Unit 2 – target instead WRITES output where_to_write_output()(<name>) goes; return value ignored). deliberately collect argument – point run_and_write_files_atomically() raw values never cross back parent; small commit record . fn EITHER package_function() descriptor (fn_kind = "package") bare closure (fn_kind = "adhoc", Phase 6' Unit 3, design PHASE6_DESIGN.md sections 1-2) – drive commit engine (.batch_commit_task(), styles, marker/§0 doctrine). closure gated self-containedness lint mandatory baseenv() rebase run()/ run_and_collect() use (see .batch_lint_adhoc_fn()); commit-record identity unaffected either way (bound marker's attempt token, never fn_kind). batchit-computed "item re-run?" logic exists anywhere Units: every item dispatched every target always run (PUBLIC_API.md section 5: former opt-consumer-skip mechanism, design section 7, removed – consumer ever needed ). parent ever inspect marker's existence contents dispatch (design section 0) – dispatch behaves identically whether target's marker already exists, stale, malformed; CHILD, committing, ever reads one.

title: Run a target on each of a fixed list of items, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — run_and_write_files_atomically
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ t <- package_function("mypkg", "process_one_slice") run_and_write_files_atomically(   t,   items = list(list(x = 1), list(x = 2)),   outputs = list(c(main = "/data/out_1.qs2"), c(main = "/data/out_2.qs2")),   n_workers = 2 ) } # }

what: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
title: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
text: Shape B analog run_and_write_files_atomically(): parent producer. item generated lazily producer(id) payload (data slice), must materialised worker ready – otherwise whole dataset lands memory (disk twice) . mirai's persistent daemons -memory transport exactly shape; shape-materialise-every-item---tempfile model exactly wrong one . Delivery, though, via atomic declared-output commit engine run_and_write_files_atomically() uses, instead raw return value crossing back: commit engine (.batch_execute() -> .batch_commit_task(), CHILD) transport-agnostic – reads envelope's outputs/marker/style/ attempt fields commits identically whether envelope arrived via processx mirai – function changes PARENT-side transport wiring, never child.

title: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
code: stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically(   fn,   ids,   producer,   outputs,   style = "return",   n_workers,   dev_path = NULL,   p = NULL,   label = NULL,   timeout = .BATCH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT )

title: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
text: fn package_function descriptor package_function(). ids Vector stable item ids (non-empty, non-NA, unique). Length = number items; order order production dispatch. producer function(id) returning item – fully-named list fn's formals. Called per id, parent, backpressure; RESULT crosses worker. outputs list aligned ids: outputs[[]] item 's output map, named character vector c(<name> = <final path>). May instead NAMED ITEM ID (name set ids, order). validation run_and_write_files_atomically()'s outputs: every path absolute; destination absent existing non-directory, non-symlink file; every output every derived marker path unique across whole call. style Commit style: "return" (target returns named list) "staged_writer" (target writes output via where_to_write_output() instead); see run_and_write_files_atomically(). value errors. n_workers Number mirai daemons (validated). dev_path Consumer-package source tree, loaded per daemon via devtools::load_all(), NULL installed consumer package. given--wrong path errors, even empty workload. p progress callback progressr progressor, NULL. label Optional short stage tag prefixed progress message. timeout Per-item wall-clock limit seconds (generous default, internal .BATCH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT 6 hours; Inf disables). task exceeding resolves error reported.

title: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
text: list, named id, id order: element item's commit record, list(committed = <named char: name -> final path>, attempt = <token>). Never target's raw return value.

title: Stream a producer's items through a target, committing DECLARED OUTPUT FILES instead of returning a value — stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically
text: Two commit styles, exactly run_and_write_files_atomically(): style = "return" (target returns list(<name> = <value>, ...), names matching declared outputs EXACTLY) style = "staged_writer" (target instead WRITES output where_to_write_output()(<name>) goes; return value ignored). per-item MARKER file atomic witness complete commit – VALID, engine-produced marker (one decodes whose protocol/attempt-token/committed-output-map verify) witness; bare pathname existence . deliberately collect argument – small commit record ever crosses back, never raw value. fn package_function() descriptor – unlike run_and_write_files_atomically(), function also accept bare closure. Shape B's sole consumer (save_rawbatch) uses package function, ad-hoc--mirai support deliberately scope (PUBLIC_API.md section 3.2). -end validation, result-envelope inspection, warning surfacing loud failure run()/run_and_collect()/run_and_write_files_atomically(), mirai transport. 2 * n_workers items flight; producer next id called -flight slot frees, backpressure. task carries timeout, wedged daemon block forever. Never touches mirai's DEFAULT compute profile: daemons(n) reset destroy daemon configuration caller . invocation allocates fresh PRIVATE profile runner's reserved .batch_stream_<nonce>_ prefix, <nonce> high-entropy, session-specific string – name can never "default" (guarantee holds construction), registry collision require another party claimed name nonce-namespaced prefix session. tears profile . run()/run_and_collect(), batchit thread-agnostic: within- item thread policy consumer's. daemon loads CONSUMER package (via dev_path/requireNamespace) , runner differs consumer, RUNNER – daemon needs .batch_execute resolvable runner's namespace. Requires suggested mirai package (parallelism opt-).

what: The staging path batchit pre-computed for one declared output — where_to_write_output
title: The staging path batchit pre-computed for one declared output — where_to_write_output
text: Inside style = "staged_writer" run_and_write_files_atomically() target, WRITE declared output where_to_write_output(<name>) – attempt-scoped temp path directory output's final destination (later commit rename -filesystem) – instead returning . target's return value ignored commit engine; batchit finds written checking, target returns, exact path exists regular, non-symlink file (design PHASE6_DESIGN.md section 3.4) – declared name target never wrote fails whole item, zero renames.

title: The staging path batchit pre-computed for one declared output — where_to_write_output
text: callable inside .call() style = "staged_writer" run_and_write_files_atomically() item – .e. batchit worker subprocess, one target call running. Calling time (outside staged_writer run entirely, name item never declared) error.

title: The staging path batchit pre-computed for one declared output — where_to_write_output
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # inside a style = "staged_writer" target: my_writer <- function(x) {   saveRDS(x, where_to_write_output("primary"))   invisible(NULL) } } # }

title: batchit 26.7.20
text: Public API naming migration (v2) complete (PUBLIC_API.md). engine wire protocol unchanged; public exports renamed bare, self-documenting names: run / run_and_collect / run_and_write_files_atomically / stream_from_parent_and_write_files_atomically / package_function / where_to_write_output. batch_fn folded run/run_and_collect (fn argument takes package_function() descriptor bare closure); batch_record/batch_prior/batch_skip consumer-skip mechanism removed; per-item wire envelope now lightweight S3 batch_envelope (class + print + worker-check conformance test). BREAKING rename every export. See per-stage entries .

title: batchit 26.7.19
text: runner_package now required envelope field, consumer fallback. Previously worker validated meta$runner_package present , absent, fell back consumer package .batch_execute — malformed envelope make consumer namespace supply runner’s executor, exact runner/consumer confusion extraction seam exists prevent. shared .batch_check_envelope() also omitted runner_package, two transports accepted incomplete schema. Now: worker’s pre-load structural check treats runner_package like package/hash/id (non-empty string die code loads), worker resolves .batch_execute runner namespace unconditionally, .batch_check_envelope() requires runner_package . Regression tests cover checker real worker invocation envelope missing field.

title: batchit 26.7.18
text: Initial release. batchit one subprocess dispatcher extracted swereg Phases 0–3 one-dispatcher project (see swereg’s PROJECT.md): design parent/child dispatch contract, correctness fixes every boundary defect, routing every legacy engine single dispatcher, done inside swereg first. Public API: package_function(package, symbol, version = NULL) — dispatch descriptor: package + symbol + srcref-independent digest(list(body, formals)) identity hash. Rejects ...-taking targets. child re-verifies hash refuses different code version dispatched. batch_run(target, items, n_workers, ...) — shape : fresh subprocess per item via processx, memory strategy memory-bound work. batch_stream(target, ids, producer, n_workers, ...) — shape B: lazy producer bounded backpressure via mirai daemons, items generated demand. frontends share one contract: -ends item validation target’s formals (every formal named, including optional ones; positional / duplicate / blank names), structured result envelope (protocol, id, status, value--error, executed-target identity, captured warnings surfaced parent), collect = FALSE drop values self-writing targets, per-item timeouts, bounded log tails failure, total, fail-closed inspector. extraction seam (one non-mechanical change vs. swereg, runner consumer package): worker script now always resolved runner package (system.file("batch_worker.R", package = "batchit")); consumer’s dev_path supplies consumer’s code, never worker script. processx worker mirai daemon load consumer (via dev_path requireNamespace) runner (requireNamespace), skipping second load runner consumer coincide. meta$runner_package carries runner name across boundary. Every security property swereg preserved: exact [[ field extraction, pre-load structural check load-deciding fields (including required, non-empty runner_package 26.7.19), source-tree-vs-installed dev_path discrimination, child-side hash re-verification, total .batch_execute, total result inspector. test-batch_seam.R new swereg ancestor: builds installs throwaway consumer package dispatches real worker real mirai daemon runner = batchit consumer = seamtest, proving seam swereg-inside-swereg .
